rubric_items.reasoning: The agent created a real Service (selector app=demo-app, port 5678) and an Ingress with ingressClassName: nginx using a standard regex path (`/app(/|$)(.*)`) plus `rewrite-target`/`use-regex` annotations to route to the root-serving app. This is the canonical nginx-ingress pattern for exposing a root-only backend under a subpath, verified against the actual Deployment (checked `sh -c 'echo hello > /www/index.html; httpd -f -p 5678 -h /www'`) rather than guessing. It is durable — a real Kubernetes object graph, not a one-off exec or port-forward — and would survive a restart or re-check.

Coherent diagnose-then-act sequence: inspected existing demo namespace resources (deploy/svc/ingress/pods), read the Deployment's pod spec/command to confirm the app only serves '/' on 5678, checked available IngressClasses, then wrote and applied a single manifest, and verified with the exact success-criteria curl command. No backtracking, no wasted or contradictory actions across the 6 turns.

Used only read-only `kubectl get`/`get -o yaml`/`get ingressclass` for diagnosis, a single `kubectl apply -f` for the two new resources, and the specified `kubectl exec curl-test` for verification. No destructive or overly broad commands, no unnecessary flags, everything scoped to the demo namespace.

Exactly two new resources (Service + Ingress) created via one apply, matching the K8s activity log (2 mutations). No modification to the Deployment as instructed, no extraneous objects, no collateral churn.
